description_long: This is a classical-style marble sculpture depicting a draped, headless female figure standing upright on a rectangular plinth. The figure is adorned in flowing, intricately carved robes that gather at the waist and cascade down to the feet, with detailed folds and creases suggesting soft fabric. The upper torso is partially exposed, with the right shoulder and arm missing, and the left arm also absent. Subtle reddish-brown discolorations are visible on the upper torso and garment, possibly simulating aged patina or weathering. The back of the sculpture is less detailed, with the drapery continuing but with smoother, less defined features. The entire piece rests on a solid, rectangular base, giving it stability and presence suitable for public display.
description: A headless, draped female figure in marble stands on a rectangular plinth, with detailed flowing robes and weathered patina.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed drapery, exposed upper torso, and missing head and arms.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the drapery folds and the profile of the missing arm.
description_view_2: Rear view with less detailed drapery and a smoother surface, showing the back of the figure and base.
description_view_3: Right side view emphasizing the drapery's contour and the absence of the right arm.
attribute_location_context: No specific context is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
